objective: Triage HELLO-BUG-002 — reproduce, locate, decide route.
key_changes: Reproduced: `python3 hello.py Alice --formal --shout` emits `Greetings, Alice` (not shouted); `--shout` alone emits `HELLO, ALICE!`.
Located defect at hello.py:15 — guard `if shout and not formal:` suppresses upper-casing whenever --formal is set.
Correct guard is `if shout:` so shouting applies to both formal and informal greetings.
Classified root cause as business-rule (conditional encodes the wrong rule for when shouting applies).
Expected shouted formal output is `GREETINGS, ALICE` (formal greeting has no trailing `!`).
Path A criterion 1 (severity ∈ {minor}) FAILS — bug severity is `major`; all other Path A criteria pass.
Per triage.md, any failed criterion forces Path B; routed to B.
findings: Root cause: hello.py:15 guard `if shout and not formal:` skips `greeting.upper()` when --formal is set, so the formal greeting is never shouted.
Reproduction: `python3 hello.py Alice --formal --shout` -> `Greetings, Alice` (expected `GREETINGS, ALICE`); control `python3 hello.py Alice --shout` -> `HELLO, ALICE!`.
Criterion 2 — fix contained in a single file: PASS (only hello.py).
Criterion 3 — diff ≤ ~20 lines: PASS (one-line change on line 15).
Criterion 4 — no schema/API/migration/security/build-system change: PASS (pure CLI logic edit).
Criterion 5 — regression test obvious from reproduction: PASS (assert `hello Alice --formal --shout` == `GREETINGS, ALICE`).
Rationale: Path A requires ALL criteria; severity is `major` not `minor`, so criterion 1 fails and Path B is mandatory.
